author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
# Marketplace installs of pm-claude silently degrade to `npx @unbrained/pm-cli@latest` — the "native" server only exists in a repo checkout

Researched against the official Claude Code plugin docs as fetched 2026-07-11 (code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference + /en/plugin-marketplaces), local Claude Code v2.1.207.

## What is actually fine (verified)

- `claude plugin validate ./plugins/pm-claude` and `claude plugin validate .` (marketplace) both PASS, including `--strict`. The directory layout (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json` + root-level `commands/ skills/ agents/ hooks/ .mcp.json`) matches the current spec exactly.
- `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` schema is valid: `name: "pm"`, owner, relative `source: "./plugins/pm-claude"`.
- Hook shape (`hooks/hooks.json`, SessionStart, `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, timeout, async) is per spec.

So structurally it IS a real Claude Code plugin. The defect is in what runs after install.

## The defect

Per the official docs, marketplace-installed plugins are **copied into `~/.claude/plugins/cache`** and "cannot reference files outside their directory" (Plugin caching and file resolution). The plugin dir ships NO dist/, NO node_modules, NO package.json — only wrapper scripts.

`plugins/pm-claude/scripts/pm-mcp-server.mjs` resolution order:
1. `process.env.PM_CLI_MCP_SERVER` (undocumented escape hatch)
2. `findRepoServer()` — walks UP from the script dir up to 10 levels looking for `dist/mcp/server.js`. From `~/.claude/plugins/cache/pm/pm-claude/<ver>/scripts/` this can never find the repo build.
3. Fallback: `spawn("npx", ["-y", "--package=@unbrained/pm-cli@latest", "pm-mcp"])`.

`plugins/pm-claude/hooks/session-start.mjs` doesn't even try a local resolution — it always runs `npx -y --package=@unbrained/pm-cli@latest pm context --limit 5 --json` (15s timeout).

**Every marketplace user is therefore on path 3**: network-dependent npx download at MCP server start + at every SessionStart, while `.mcp.json` advertises "Imports pm command modules directly — no pm shell invocation needed" and the manifest says "Native pm CLI integration". The native path only works for people who cloned this repo (where the project-root `.mcp.json` is what actually serves the tools — not the plugin).

## Compounding version-skew defect

- `plugin.json` pins `"version": "1.4.1"` (bumped once, 2026-07-02, never since — npm has shipped daily date-based releases up to 2026.7.11). Docs: pinned version means users NEVER receive plugin updates until the field is bumped; pushing commits does nothing.
- Docs also warn: version set in BOTH `plugin.json` and the marketplace entry → plugin.json silently wins ("a stale manifest version can mask a version you set in marketplace.json"). We set it in both.
- Meanwhile the npx fallback tracks npm `@latest`. Net: frozen 1.4.1 skills/commands/hooks driving an ever-moving CLI — the skill/tool contract can drift arbitrarily.

## Officially documented fix pattern (verbatim from plugins-reference, 2026-07-11)

`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` is "a persistent directory for plugin state that survives updates. Use this for installed dependencies such as node_modules". Recommended recipe (documented under "Persistent data directory"):

1. SessionStart hook: `diff -q "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json" "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json" || (cd "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}" && cp "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json" . && npm install) || rm -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json"` — installs on first run and re-installs only when a plugin update changes the dependency manifest.
2. Ship a `package.json` in the plugin pinning `@unbrained/pm-cli` to the exact version the plugin snapshot was built against.
3. `.mcp.json`: `"command": "node", "args": ["${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/pm-mcp-server.mjs"], "env": {"NODE_PATH": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules"}` — wrapper imports the SDK server from NODE_PATH (true in-process native server, offline after first session).
4. session-start.mjs resolves the same installed copy instead of npx@latest.
5. Version strategy — pick ONE: (a) release automation bumps `plugin.json` version in lockstep with the npm release (single source; drop the marketplace-entry copy), or (b) omit `version` everywhere so the git commit SHA versions the plugin (docs recommend this for actively-developed plugins — every push is an update).

## Repro

1. `/plugin marketplace add unbraind/pm-cli` ; `/plugin install pm-claude@pm` on a machine WITHOUT this repo checked out.
2. Observe MCP server start = npx download of @unbrained/pm-cli@latest (fails entirely offline); SessionStart hook likewise.
3. `claude plugin details pm-claude` reports 1.4.1 while the tools served come from npm latest.

## Also noticed (spot-check, works as intended)

- `scripts/smoke-claude-plugin.mjs` validates structure + repo-mode server only; it never exercises the installed/cache layout, which is why the fallback path has no coverage.
- Root `marketplace.json` duplicate is intentional legacy compat (asserted by the smoke test).

Sources: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference (Persistent data directory, Plugin caching, Version management), https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces (Version resolution and release channels).
Claude plugin: marketplace installs fall back to npx @latest, not the advertised native MCP server

Marketplace/cache install serves MCP tools via in-process import from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} install (no npx at server start; offline after first session); SessionStart hook uses the same pinned local install instead of npx @latest; Single version source: release automation bumps plugin.json in lockstep with the npm release OR version omitted everywhere for SHA-based updates; marketplace-entry version duplicate removed; Smoke test exercises the installed/cache layout (no dist/ ancestor) and asserts the native path, not the npx fallback; docs/CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN.md and plugin README updated to match actual resolution behavior
Restores the plugin's core promise (native, offline-capable, version-coherent) for every external marketplace user
Verified against official docs as of 2026-07-11; gap widens with every daily npm release while plugin.json stays at 1.4.1
Claude Code v2.1.207, marketplace install path (~/.claude/plugins/cache)

/plugin marketplace add unbraind/pm-cli; /plugin install pm-claude@pm on a machine without the repo checkout; start a session and observe the MCP server + SessionStart hook shell out to npx -y @unbrained/pm-cli@latest (network-dependent), while claude plugin details reports pinned 1.4.1
Marketplace-installed plugin serves the native in-process MCP server from a version-pinned local install (offline after first session), matching the manifest claim 'no pm shell invocation needed'

findRepoServer() cannot resolve dist/mcp/server.js from the plugin cache, so every installed user runs the npx @latest fallback for both the MCP server and the SessionStart hook; plugin.json pins 1.4.1 (never bumped since 2026-07-02) while the fallback tracks npm latest — unbounded contract skew

message: Filed from Claude-plugin implementation research vs official docs 2026-07-11

value.note: duplicate version pin; plugin.json silently wins per docs
documents the npx fallback as acceptable; update with fix
claims native import / no shell invocation
fallback chain: PM_CLI_MCP_SERVER -> findRepoServer (never matches in plugin cache) -> npx @latest

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Official-docs digest (fetched 2026-07-11, code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference + /en/plugin-marketplaces, Claude Code v2.1.207) — key spec facts this item relies on, so nobody has to re-fetch:
(1) Marketplace plugins are COPIED to ~/.claude/plugins/cache; paths outside the plugin dir never resolve after install ("Path traversal limitations").
(2) ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} = persistent per-plugin dir (~/.claude/plugins/data/<id>/) surviving updates; the documented dependency pattern is a SessionStart hook that diffs the bundled package.json against a copy in DATA and npm-installs there, then servers run with NODE_PATH=${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules. Data dir is auto-deleted on last-scope uninstall (--keep-data preserves).
(3) Version resolution order: plugin.json version > marketplace-entry version > git commit SHA > "unknown". Pinning means users update ONLY on a field bump; docs explicitly warn against setting it in both files (plugin.json silently masks marketplace) and recommend omitting version entirely for actively-developed plugins so every commit is an update.
(4) ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} changes on every update; old version dirs linger ~7 days then are purged — never store state there. Mid-session updates keep old paths until /reload-plugins.
(5) plugin.json: only `name` required; unrecognized top-level fields are ignored (warnings under `claude plugin validate --strict`); wrong TYPES still fail. New useful fields: displayName (2.1.143+), defaultEnabled (2.1.154+), $schema, dependencies with semver, userConfig (prompted at enable, exported as CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_<KEY> and ${user_config.*}).
(6) commands/ = legacy flat-file skills; docs say use skills/ for new plugins. Components must sit at plugin root, NOT inside .claude-plugin/ (only plugin.json lives there).
(7) Marketplace: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json at repo root; relative sources resolve against marketplace root; metadata.description/version accepted only for backward compat; `renames` map (2.1.193+) for renaming/removing plugins without breaking installs; reserved-name list exists for official-sounding marketplace names.
(8) Bundled-MCP hook matchers must use scoped names mcp__plugin_<plugin>_<server>__<tool>; bare server keys never fire — relevant if we ever add PreToolUse hooks around pm_* tools.

author: maintainer-agent
message: clear ACs for rewrite after version-policy change (pm-hxsv)

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Marketplace/cache install serves MCP tools via in-process import from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} install (no npx at server start; offline after first session); SessionStart hook uses the same pinned local install instead of npx @latest; DONE via pm-hxsv (2026-07-11): plugin/marketplace/package versions are date-based and release-automation keeps them in lockstep with the root package (scripts/sync-versions.mjs apply in pipeline + version:check CI gate); duplicate version fields retained deliberately but always identical; Plugin ships a package.json pinning @unbrained/pm-cli to the exact version the snapshot was built against, so served tools match the plugin's skills/commands; Smoke test exercises the installed/cache layout (no dist/ ancestor) and asserts the native path, not the npx fallback; docs/CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN.md and plugin README updated to match actual resolution behavior

message: AC #3 resolved by pm-hxsv; added explicit dependency-pin AC

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Partial progress via pm-hxsv (closed 2026-07-11): the version-skew half is fixed at the policy level — all plugin/marketplace/package manifests now carry the root date-based version (2026.7.11), the release pipeline re-stamps them on every cut, and the CI static gate rejects drift. Effect: marketplace users receive a plugin update with every release instead of being frozen at 1.4.1. STILL OPEN (core of this item): the npx@latest fallback itself — plugin must bundle a pinned package.json + ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} install so the served MCP server matches the plugin version and works offline; date-lockstep makes that pin trivial (plugin version == npm version by construction).
